block_00: Take a look at the top news of the week
block_03: Amazon becomes the world’s first public company to lose $1 trillion in market value. Shares in the e-commerce and cloud company fell 4.3% this Wednesday, pushing its market value to about $879 billion from a record close of $1.88 trillion on July 2021.
block_07: Some of the biggest stock-market winners after the CPI report Thursday morning are shares of technology companies that had been battered this year. Meta Platforms shares jumped more than 4%, outperforming the broader market – plus 21% this week.
block_10: California dumps the proposal to tax the rich for EVs and green initiatives. Lyft was the most prominent financier of Proposition 30, contributing more than 97% of the roughly $50 million raised to promote it – the share price has fallen by 19% in one week.
block_14: Stay tuned for emails, so you don't miss all the updates that are coming this year.
